title: Hungarian Wine Climate Atlas — district-level climate susceptibility, variety suitability and threats for the 22 Hungarian wine districts (1971–2100)
description:
An open-source bilingual (Hungarian + English) climate-suitability atlas for the 22 Hungarian wine districts (borvidék), 1971–2100. Three peer-reviewed climate datasets (FORESEE v1.1 CNRM-ALADIN53 regional projection, the full 14-member FORESEE EURO-CORDEX ensemble, and the World Bank CCKP CMIP6 0.25° ensemble) are joined to a curated envelope bank of 81 grape varieties and surfaced both as a publishable Next.js + MapLibre + deck.gl static site and as a reproducibility bundle for the associated research paper.

Contents:

  • analysis/ — Python pipeline that extracts daily climate fields via area-weighted district polygon means, computes nine viticulture indices (Winkler GDD, Huglin Index, BEDD, AGST, cool-night index, Hargreaves PET drought balance, late-spring-frost and heat-day counts), evaluates multi-factor variety suitability with a cos²-centred Huglin score across four future bins (2021–2040, 2041–2060, 2061–2080, 2081–2100) under RCP4.5 and RCP8.5, and cross-checks against the CCKP CMIP6 ensemble.
  • paper_analysis/ — the manuscript reproducibility bundle: full s00–s15 pipeline scripts, figure scripts, 81-variety envelope, all main and appendix figures (PNG + PDF), manuscript tables as CSV, and all underlying numerical results as CSV (annual indices, per-horizon suitability, 14-member EURO-CORDEX ensemble aggregates plus each member individually).
  • analysis/curated/ — the publishable data bundle: 22-district GeoJSON, per-district climate normals, per-district variety suitability (the site uses 57 of the 81 rows as the high-confidence subset), CCKP extracts, HungaroMet station observations, curated threats (2025 Flavescence dorée outbreak, grapevine trunk diseases, late spring frost, EU pesticide status), manifest.json and SHA-256 checksums.
  • site/ — bilingual Next.js static site with 1,091 prerendered pages: per-district detail pages, 462 side-by-side district comparisons, threat-ranking views, methods explainers, downloadable per-district A4 PDFs, and a sortable comparison table.
  • research/ — 22 long-form district dossiers (~123,000 words) and two synthesis briefs, fully translated to Hungarian.

Audiences: Hungarian and international wine investors evaluating district-level climate risk; growers and hegyközségi tanácsadók considering variety adaptation; climate-impact researchers needing a reproducible Hungarian baseline; reviewers of the associated manuscript; policy makers and the wine-curious public.

Reproducibility: Every site chart and every manuscript figure and table can be re-derived from the curated parquet/JSON/GeoJSON in analysis/curated/ and from the CSV mirrors in paper_analysis/data/. The Python pipeline is idempotent and re-runnable; the Next.js site builds to a static export.
